sections.label: Section 1333
sections.translation: Woe ! woe to you , you child of Tyndareus ,

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.translation: for the suffering and anguish sore , which you are causing the Danaids !

sections.label: Section 1336
sections.original: ἐγὼ μὲν οἰκτίρω σε συμφορᾶς κακῆς
sections.translation: I pity you for your cruel fate — a fate I would you never had met !

sections.label: Section 1338
sections.original: ὦ τεκοῦσα μῆτερ , ἀνδρῶν ὄχλον εἰσορῶ πέλας .
sections.translation: O mother that bore me ! I see a throng of men approaching .

sections.label: Section 1339
sections.original: τόν τε τῆς θεᾶς παῖδα , τέκνον , ᾧ σὺ δεῦρʼ ἐλήλυθας .
sections.translation: It is the goddess ’ son you see , child , for whom you came here .

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: διαχαλᾶτέ μοι μέλαθρα , δμῶες , ὡς κρύψω δέμας .
sections.translation: Open the tent-door to me , servants , that I may hide myself

sections.label: Section 1341
sections.translation: Why seek to escape , my child ?

sections.label: Section 1341b
sections.translation: I am ashamed to face Achilles .

sections.label: Section 1342
sections.translation: But why ?

sections.label: Section 1342b
sections.original: τὸ δυστυχές μοι τῶν γάμων αἰδῶ φέρει .
sections.translation: The luckless ending to our marriage causes me to feel abashed .

sections.label: Section 1343
sections.original: οὐκ ἐν ἁβρότητι κεῖσαι πρὸς τὰ νῦν πεπτωκότα .
sections.translation: No time for affectation now in face of what has happened . Stay then ; reserve will do no good , if we may profit . [*]

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.translation: Daughter of Leda , lady of sorrows !

sections.label: Section 1345b
sections.translation: No misnomer that .

sections.label: Section 1346
sections.translation: A fearful cry is heard among the Argives .

sections.label: Section 1346b
sections.translation: What is it ? tell me .

sections.label: Section 1347
sections.translation: It concerns your child .

sections.label: Section 1347b
sections.translation: An evil omen for your words .

sections.label: Section 1348
sections.translation: They say her sacrifice is necessary .

sections.label: Section 1348b
sections.translation: And is there no one to say a word against them ?

sections.label: Section 1349
sections.original: ἐς θόρυβον ἐγώ τι καὐτὸς ἤλυθον —
sections.translation: Indeed I was in some danger myself from the tumult .

sections.label: Section 1349b
sections.translation: In danger of what , stranger ?.

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.translation: Of being stoned .

sections.label: Section 1350b
sections.translation: Surely not for trying to save my daughter ?

sections.label: Section 1351
sections.translation: The very reason .

sections.label: Section 1351b
sections.original: τίς δʼ ἂν ἔτλη τοῦ σώματος τοῦ σοῦ θιγεῖν ;
sections.translation: Who would have dared to lay a finger on you ?

sections.label: Section 1352
sections.translation: All the men of Hellas .

sections.label: Section 1352b
sections.original: στρατὸς δὲ Μυρμιδὼν οὔ σοι παρῆν ;
sections.translation: Were not your Myrmidon warriors at your side ?

sections.label: Section 1353
sections.translation: They were the first who turned against me .

sections.label: Section 1353b
sections.translation: My child ! we are lost , it seems .

sections.label: Section 1354
sections.original: οἵ με τὸν γάμων ἀπεκάλουν ἥσσονα .
sections.translation: They taunted me as the man whom marriage had enslaved .

sections.label: Section 1354b
sections.translation: And what did you answer them ?

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.original: τὴν ἐμὴν μέλλουσαν εὐνὴν μὴ κτανεῖν —
sections.translation: Not to kill the one I meant to wed —

sections.label: Section 1355b
sections.translation: Justly so .

sections.label: Section 1356
sections.translation: The wife her father promised me .

sections.label: Section 1356b
sections.translation: Yes , and sent to fetch from Argos .

sections.label: Section 1357
sections.translation: But I was overcome by clamorous cries .

sections.label: Section 1357b
sections.translation: Truly the mob is a dire mischief .

sections.label: Section 1358
sections.translation: But I will help you for all that .

sections.label: Section 1358b
sections.translation: Will you really fight them single-handed ?

sections.label: Section 1359
sections.translation: Do you see these warriors here , carrying my arms ?

sections.label: Section 1359b
sections.translation: Bless you for your kind intent !

sections.label: Section 1360
sections.translation: Well , I shall be blessed .

sections.label: Section 1360b
sections.translation: Then my child will not be slaughtered now ?

sections.label: Section 1361
sections.translation: No , not with my consent at any rate .

sections.label: Section 1361b
sections.translation: But will any of them come to lay hands on the maid ?

sections.label: Section 1362
sections.translation: Thousands of them , with Odysseus at their head .

sections.label: Section 1362b
sections.translation: The son of Sisyphus ?

sections.label: Section 1363
sections.translation: The very same .

sections.label: Section 1363b
sections.original: ἴδια πράσσων , ἢ στρατοῦ ταχθεὶς ὕπο ;
sections.translation: Acting for himself or by the army ’ s order ?

sections.label: Section 1364
sections.translation: By their choice — and his own .

sections.label: Section 1364b
sections.translation: An evil choice indeed , to stain his hands in blood .

sections.label: Section 1365
sections.translation: But I will hold him back .

sections.label: Section 1365b
sections.translation: Will he seize and bear her off against her will ?

sections.label: Section 1366
sections.translation: Yes , by her golden hair no doubt .

sections.label: Section 1366b
sections.original: ἐμὲ δὲ δρᾶν τί χρὴ τότε ;
sections.translation: What must I do , when it comes to that ?

sections.label: Section 1367
sections.translation: Keep hold of your daughter .

sections.label: Section 1367b
sections.translation: Be sure that she shall not be slain , as far as that that can help her .

sections.label: Section 1368
sections.original: ἀλλὰ μὴν ἐς τοῦτό γʼ ἥξει .
sections.translation: Believe me , it will come to this . [*]

sections.label: Section 1368b
sections.translation: Mother , hear me while I speak , for I see that you are angry with your husband

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: σῷ πόσει · τὰ δʼ ἀδύναθʼ ἡμῖν καρτερεῖν οὐ ῥᾴδιον .
sections.translation: to no purpose ; it is hard for us to persist in impossibilities . Our thanks are due to this stranger for his ready help ; but you must also see to it that he is not reproached by the army , leaving us no better off and himself involved in trouble .
